title: Quantifying motor function using beg signals
abstract: A motor control score is automatically generated for a subject based on electroencephalography (EEG) data represented as a plurality of EEG waveforms obtained from a plurality of EEG electrodes. Each waveform is separated into at least one window, containing a portion of the EEG waveform representative of neural activity corresponding to a movement performed by the subject. At least one or more frequency components and/or signal components are determined from the EEG data in the window, and a motor control score is determined based on these components. The frequency components may correspond to event-related desynchronization (ERD) response frequency band and event-related synchronization (ERS) response frequency band. In other embodiments, the frequency components correspond to a phase response and the signal components correspond to a signal power spectrum density of the window, which are provided to a primary neural network model to determine the motor control score.
claims:
Claims A computer-implemented method to automatically generate a motor control score for a subject based on electroencephalography (EEG) data represented as a plurality of EEG waveforms obtained from a plurality of EEG electrodes, the method comprising: (a) for each EEG waveform of the plurality of EEG waveforms, separating the EEG waveform into at least one window, wherein each window contains a portion of the EEG waveform representative of neural activity corresponding to a movement performed by the subject; (b) determining, from the EEG data in the at least one window, at least one or more frequency components; and (c) calculating the motor control score based on the at least one or more frequency components. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein the one or more frequency components correspond to at least one event-related desynchronization (ERD) response frequency band and at least one event- related synchronization (ERS) response frequency band; the motor control score is calculated based on at least one ERD and ERS waveform feature extracted from the plurality of EEG waveforms based on the at least one ERD response frequency band and the at least one ERS response frequency band; and the at least one ERD and ERS waveform feature is obtained by: 

 (a) filtering the plurality of EEG waveforms using the at least one ERD response frequency band and the at least one ERS response frequency band; (b) grouping the plurality of EEG electrodes into at least one spatial group, wherein a respective virtual EEG channel is associated with each of the at least one spatial group; (c) generating the ERD and ERS waveforms from the respective virtual EEG channel associated with each of the at least one spatial group; and (d) extracting the at least one ERD and ERS waveform features from the ERD and ERS waveforms associated with each of the at least one spatial group. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 2 or any other claim herein, wherein the at least one ERD response frequency band and the at least one ERS response frequency band is determined by: (a) calculating an average window using a set of waveform windows associated with a single EEG electrode; (b) outputting a time-frequency domain representation of the averaged window; (c) identifying, within a first frequency range of the time-frequency domain representation, the at least one ERD response frequency band; and (d) identifying, within a second frequency range of the time-frequency domain representation, the at least one ERS response frequency band. The computer-implemented method of claim 3 or any other claim herein, wherein one electrode within a spatial group is detects the same or similar ERD and ERS characteristics as at least one other electrode within the spatial group. The computer-implemented method of claim 3 or 4 or any other claim herein, wherein the first frequency range is between 8Hz and 12 Hz and the second frequency range is between 13 Hz and 30 Hz. The computer-implemented method of any one of claims 3 to 5 or any other claim herein, wherein the first frequency range and second frequency range are each between 1 Hz and 45 Hz. The computer-implemented method of any one of claims 3 to 6 or any other claim herein, wherein the time-frequency domain representation is generated using one of a Fourier transform and a wavelet transform. 8. The computer-implemented method of any one of claims 3 to 7 or any other claim herein, wherein the at least one ERD response frequency band corresponds to a low signal intensity region of the time-frequency domain representation within the first frequency range, and wherein the at least one ERS response frequency band corresponds to a high signal intensity region of the time-frequency domain representation within the second frequency range. 9. The computer-implemented method of any one of claims 3 to 8 or any other claim herein, wherein the at least one ERD response frequency band has a bandwidth of 2 Hz and the at least one ERS response frequency band has a bandwidth of 4 Hz. 10. The computer-implemented method of any one of claims 3 to 9 or any other claim herein, wherein the at least one ERD response frequency band and the at least one ERS response frequency band has a respective bandwidth that is 

 proportional to a respective size of the first frequency range and the second frequency range, respectively. 11. The computer-implemented method of claim 2 or any other claim herein, wherein the electrodes are grouped based on their respective location on the subjectâs head. 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 2 or any other claim herein, comprising outputting the virtual EEG channel signal by combining each of the at least one EEG electrodes in the spatial group based on an optimized weighting value assigned to each of the at least one EEG electrode. 13. The computer-implemented method of claim 12 or any other claim herein, wherein the optimized weighting value is determined using gradient descent to obtain a maximized relative difference in signal power between a baseline period and a movement period. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 2 or any other claim herein, wherein the step of extracting ERD and ERS waveforms comprises, for each virtual EEG channel, (a) squaring a virtual EEG waveform of the virtual EEG channel to obtain a power signal; (b) separating the power signal into at least one window, each window surrounding a portion of the power signal corresponding to an instance of a series of movements performed by the subject; (c) averaging the at least one window to generate an average window; and (d) normalizing the average window to a portion of the power signal at a baseline period, wherein the baseline period is a period of time prior to the instance of movement. 15. The computer-implemented method of claim 2 or any other claim herein, wherein the motor control score is calculated using at least one of: (a) summation of respective values of the at least one extracted ERD and ERS waveform feature; (b) subtraction of the respective values of the at least one extracted ERD and ERS waveform feature; (c) multiplication of the respective values of the at least one extracted ERD and ERS waveform feature; (d) division of the respective values of the at least one extracted ERD and ERS waveform feature; (e) scaling of the respective values of the at least one extracted ERD and ERS waveform feature by a constant; (f) normalization of the respective values of the at least one extracted ERD and ERS waveform feature relative to a known population distribution; (g) exponentiation of the respective values of the at least one extracted ERD and ERS waveform feature by raising to the power of one of a positive or a negative value; (h) scaling or multiplication of the respective values of the at least one extracted ERD and ERS waveform feature with function; and (i) transforming of the respective values of the at least one extracted ERD and ERS waveform feature by a function. 16. The computer-implemented method of claim 15 or any other claim herein, wherein the constant for scaling of the respective values of the at least one extracted ERD and ERS waveform feature comprises a known or estimated 

 correlation coefficient for that feature type with a desired motor control score output. 17. The computer-implemented method of claim 15 or any other claim herein, wherein the function for scaling or multiplication of the respective values of the at least one extracted ERD and ERS waveform feature comprises one of a sine function and cosine function. 18. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 or any other claim herein, wherein the one or more frequency components correspond to at least one phase response of the at least one window; at least one or more signal components are determined from the EEG data in the at least one window, the one or more signal components corresponding to at least one signal power spectrum density of the at least one window; and the motor control score is calculated using a primary neural network, the primary neural network being provided with the EEG data in the at least one window as input comprising at least one phase response and at least one signal power spectrum. 19. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 or any other claim herein, wherein the plurality of EEG waveforms are filtered using a 1-45 bandpass finite impulse response filter prior to the step of separating the EEG waveforms into at least one window. 20. The computer-implemented method of claim 18 or any other claim herein, wherein the phase response of the EEG data in the at least one window comprises frequency elements within at least the alpha band and the beta band. 21. The computer-implemented method of claim 18 or any other claim herein, wherein fast Fourier transform is performed on the at least one window to obtain the at least one phase response and the at least one signal power spectrum density. 22. The computer-implemented method of claim 18 or any other claim herein, wherein the primary neural network is configured using regression with a training data set having phase responses and power spectrum densities in a plurality of EEG waveforms, and a plurality of manually-assessed clinical motor function scores associated with the plurality of EEG waveforms. 23. The computer-implemented method of claim 18 or any other claim herein, wherein the at least one phase response and the at least one signal power spectrum density is calculated from a corresponding frequency domain representation of the at least one window. 24. The computer-implemented method of claim 18 or any other claim herein, wherein the primary neural network model is configured using a configuration framework comprising at least two two-dimensional convolutional neural network layers, at least two max pooling layers, and at least two fully connected layers. 25. The computer-implemented method of claim 18 or any other claim herein, wherein the primary neural network model is configured using a configuration framework comprising a two-dimensional convolutional neural network layer and a fully connected layer. 26. Apparatus having any new and inventive feature, combination of features, or sub-combination of features as described herein. 27. Methods having any new and inventive steps, acts, combination of steps and/or acts or sub-combination of steps and/or acts as described herein.
